expected_behaviors: Identifies 3 deferred findings as incomplete work
Classifies status as Active, not Completed
Does NOT accept report's self-assessment of complete at face value
Accurately counts 3/6 tasks complete
Classifies as Active
References review cycle count
Does NOT round up completion percentage
Identifies missing Task 3 in plan-adherence audit
Does NOT claim full plan adherence
Lists the specific missing task with its description

test_cases.prompt:
Summarize the status and outcomes of this implementation plan.

{fixture:4-dirty-app-plan-summary}
expected_behaviors: Identifies that only 4 of 6 tasks are completed (not all as claimed)
Flags the discrepancy: team lead claims 'all critical security issues addressed' but SSRF fix was deferred
Notes that PII scrubbing was deferred with weak justification
Does NOT accept the claimed outcome at face value
Identifies Task 3 rate limiting as incomplete — _apply_rate_limit is a stub that always returns None, so no actual rate limiting is implemented despite the claim of completion
Identifies Task 5 test coverage as incomplete — all 4 test functions assert result is None, validating the stub's return value rather than testing any real rate-limiting behavior
Does not flag Tasks 1, 2, 4, or 6 as incomplete — those tasks contain actual implementation code and passing tests that verify real behavior
Distinguishes between 'claimed complete' and 'actually complete' based on code snippets: Tasks 3 and 5 form a pair where a stub is implemented and tested as if it were real
Correctly interprets 'deferred to the architect' as consultation, not work deferral
Correctly interprets 'active directory integration' as a completed feature (AD product), not an active status
Correctly interprets 'no missing dependencies' as all dependencies present, not something missing
Does not fabricate issues based on keyword matching without context
Identifies the TODO comment in Task 2 cache.py code — cache invalidation on write operations is not implemented
Identifies Task 5 test coverage gap — only 3 of 7 public methods tested in test_cache.py
Notes that Task 2 claims '50,000 cached reads per second' from Redis documentation — this is a vendor claim, not measured in this application
Notes that Task 4 cites Cloudflare blog's '95% of production APIs' statistic without verification — it is marketing content, not evidence
Flags the 'Deferred Findings: None' claim as contradicted by the TODO in Task 2 and sparse test coverage in Task 5
Classifies status as Active, not Complete — there is unfinished work (cache invalidation TODO, incomplete test coverage)
Accurately captures the primary recommendation: consolidate to single shared engine with pool_size=10, max_overflow=20
Identifies all 3 internal findings and their 1:1 mapping to recommendations 1-3
Notes that Source 2 (PgBouncer blog) is authored by the PgBouncer maintainer — potential bias in recommendation 4
Notes that Source 3 (PostgreSQL Wiki) predates PostgreSQL 16 — the 10MB-per-connection estimate may be outdated
Classifies status as Research Complete, not Implemented — no code changes were made, only recommendations documented
Does NOT fabricate additional findings or recommendations beyond what the report contains
Catches the scoring matrix contradiction: Approach B scores 7.5 weighted total, Approach A scores 7.1, but the report declares Approach A the winner
Flags that the winner selection contradicts the quantitative scoring — the rationale discusses qualitative merits that were not captured in the criteria weights
Notes the deferred item: correlation ID logging from Approach B was not backported
Classifies status as Winner Selected with a caveat about the scoring inconsistency
